summary: ai-sdk-helpers exfiltrates developer identity and GitHub CLI credentials on install
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads developer-identity and credential-adjacent files — ~/.gitconfig, the project's.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI authentication store) — together with os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, the install cwd, and CI/identity environment variables (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL, CI provider tokens). The collected payload (scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, username, cwd) is POSTed via https.request to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the declared publisher (ai-sdk.guide). README comments claiming 'fully anonymous' and 'no credentials transmitted' are contradicted by the actual payload contents. The package additionally ships scripts/publish-versions.sh, which explicitly comments 'This creates the appearance of an active, maintained package' and loops `npm publish` over 21 fabricated historical versions (0.1.0 → 1.4.1) to launder reputation. The package name and keywords ('ai-sdk', 'vercel-ai') impersonate Vercel's official `ai` / `@ai-sdk/*` namespace to capture mistaken installs. This is a deliberate developer-identity harvester with namespace-abuse cover.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
